name: Habbab ibn Amir
title: Brave Sha'bi
shortDescription: A sincere Shia from Kufa who joined the Imam's caravan after Muslim ibn Aqeel's martyrdom and fell in the first attack of Ashura.
fullStory:
Habbab ibn Amir al-Sha'bi was a brave and sincere Shia from Kufa. With a heart full of love for the Ahl al-Bayt (AS), he had pledged allegiance to Muslim ibn Aqeel during the oppressive era of Kufa. After the failure of Muslim's uprising and his martyrdom, Habbab became even more determined to rush to the aid of Imam Hussain (AS).

When he heard the news of Imam Hussain's (AS) movement, he secretly left Kufa to avoid falling into the hands of Ibn Ziyad's agents. Crossing the deserts, he reached the Hussaini caravan before it was completely surrounded.

On the day of Ashura, Habbab ibn Amir stood in the ranks of the Imam's companions with firm resolve and a martyrdom-seeking spirit. As the mass attack and the rain of arrows from the Kufan army began, he made his chest a shield.

He fought skillfully in this unequal battle and killed a number of enemy archers, but the sheer number of enemy forces and the continuous barrage of arrows deprived him of the chance to resist further.

Finally, Habbab ibn Amir fell on the scorching soil of Karbala during that very first attack on Ashura, and his lofty soul joined his martyred companions to have his name immortalized in history forever.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلى حَبابِ بْنِ عامِرٍ الشَّعْبِيِّ...
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon Habbab ibn Amir al-Sha'bi.
